faults.name: Timing capacitor reversed or wrong polarity
faults.why: A reversed electrolytic timing capacitor leaks or fails, distorting the oscillation.
faults.verification_test: Power off and verify capacitor polarity from threshold/trigger node to ground.
faults.fix_recipe: Install the timing capacitor with correct polarity and voltage rating.

faults.name: Threshold and trigger pins not tied correctly
faults.why: Astable operation depends on threshold and trigger tracking the timing capacitor node.
faults.verification_test: Check continuity between pins 2 and 6 and the timing capacitor node.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect pins 2 and 6 to the capacitor node.

faults.name: Discharge pin open
faults.why: If pin 7 is not connected through the timing resistors, the capacitor cannot discharge normally.
faults.verification_test: Probe pin 7 and confirm it switches between charge and discharge states.
faults.fix_recipe: Reconnect the discharge pin to the RA/RB junction.
